commit.subject: [ir] Move type member to Value base class
commit.message:
[ir] Move type member to Value base class

All but one subclass of Value declared its own type member and
SetType() method, as well as overriding the virtual Type()
getter. Instead, just declare the type in the base class and define
those methods in the base class too.

As well as removing an unnecessary virtual method and duplicated code
in the subclasses, this also makes it possible to call SetType() on a
value without first casting it to a subclass, which is used to
slightly simplify a callsite in ChangeImmediateToUniform.

Change-Id: Ib1f34b01fef841071e02a868a216b76cfe517f80

commit.subject: [wgsl] Support immedate function parameters
commit.message:
[wgsl] Support immedate function parameters

This should be supported if unrestricted_pointer_parameters is
supported. The backends now all support this via transforms.

Set the binding point for immediates in MSL so that they don't clash
with other buffers in the shader.

Fixed: 457501411
Change-Id: I85702abf04b4853b8d9474150ee525c705effda9

parents.subject: [ir] Support immediate in DVA

commit.subject: [ir] Handle immediate pointer params in ChangeImmediateToUniform
commit.message:
[ir] Handle immediate pointer params in ChangeImmediateToUniform

The MSL backend does not run `DirectVariableAccess`, so we cannot
assume that they will not be present.

Bug: 457501411
Change-Id: I030e7516066cf7931c1f396717c49a12222938c4

commit.subject: Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue
commit.message:
Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue

The issue was, I was unaware that there is not a one-to-one
correspondance between a BindGroupLayout and a BindGroupLayoutInternal.
Instead, a BindGroupLayoutInternal can be referenced by multiple
BindGroupLayout objects. The code making a BindGroupLayoutInternal
hold a reference to a PipelineBase to to make it possible to get
from an implicit BGL to the pipeline that created it. But that can't
happen from a BGLInternal because it could have been created by any
pipeline that had a matching layout.

The reason it was on the BGLInteral is because BGLInternal has a per
backend implementation (BindGroupLayoutInternalWGPU) where as
BindGroupLayout does not. But, BindGroupLayout is the only place that
makes sense so this association needs to be moved to the front end
because we want to get back the specific Pipeline that created
this specific BindGroupLayout.

Bug: 455605671
Change-Id: I78e866eda372df48f37e0b6b91e8d6c76199e458

commit.subject: Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue
commit.message:
Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue

The previous solution was associating pipelines
with BindGroupLayoutInternals. That was just flat
out invalid as BindGroupLayoutInternals are deduped
so the association was incorrect.

The association was needed for bindGroupLayouts
that are implicitly created by pipelines using
automatic layout as we need to know which pipeline
to create to get the implicitly generated
bindGroupLayout.

The new solution gathers all the pipelines used in
a command buffer first and serialized them. That way,
ids for the implicit bindGroupLayouts already exist
and it's safe to then serialize the bindGroups.

Bug: 455605671
Change-Id: I78e866eda372df48f37e0b6b91e8d6c76199e458

commit.subject: Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue
commit.message:
Fix WebGPU-On-WebGPU use after free issue

The previous solution was associating pipelines
with BindGroupLayoutInternals. That was just flat
out invalid as BindGroupLayoutInternals are deduped
so the association was incorrect.

The association was needed for bindGroupLayouts
that are implicitly created by pipelines using
automatic layout as we need to know which pipeline
to create to get the implicitly generated
bindGroupLayout.

The new solution gathers all the pipelines used in
a command buffer first and serialized them. That way,
ids for the implicit bindGroupLayouts already exist
and it's safe to then serialize the bindGroups.

Bug: 455605671
Change-Id: I78e866eda372df48f37e0b6b91e8d6c76199e458

parents.subject: [null] Add a simple Null backend to Tint.

commit.subject: D3D11: Cache shader objects from same shader blobs.
commit.message:
D3D11: Cache shader objects from same shader blobs.

Currently, Dawn only caches the shader *blob* (the compiled HLSL bytecode)
but not the actual D3D11 shader *objects* (ID3D11VertexShader,
ID3D11PixelShader, ID3D11ComputeShader). The creation of these shader
objects from the blob can be a non-trivial operation, impacting performance.

This change introduces caching for the D3D11 shader objects themselves.
The caching mechanism uses the SHA3 hash of the shader blob as the key
to store and retrieve the D3D11 shader objects. This allows for reuse
when multiple pipelines use the same underlying shader blob, thereby
reducing redundant object creation and improving performance.

Bug: 439845637, chromium:40266176
Change-Id: I15e2b74b869d96ee890d38647405fc288f4d94e2

commit.message:
Create SharedTextureMemory from D3D12 resource

Adds support for creating SharedTextureMemory from a D3D12Resource
without the need for a shared handle.

Adds support for creating SharedTextureMemory from a D3D12Resource
without the need for a shared handle. This is done by recycling the
SharedBufferMemoryD3D12ResourceDescriptor during creation of the Shared
Texture Memory.

commit.subject: Expose the 11On12 device and Command Queue via backend
commit.message:
Expose the 11On12 device and Command Queue via backend

This change makes Dawn's D3D12 command queue and D3D11On12 device
available to Chromium via the D3D12Backend interface. The command queue
and device will be used to support D3D12 resource based Direct
Composition textures.

Change-Id: I9030b34aa0572130922de8c45fad8b8d936b9380

commit.subject: [spirv] Support zero-value construct for all types
commit.message:
[spirv] Support zero-value construct for all types

This cannot happen when coming from WGSL because they will be turned
into constants, but the fuzzer can create them and other non-WGSL
flows potentially could too. Emit them as OpConstantNull, which is
what we already did for zero-value subgroup-matrix construct
instructions.

Fixed: 456476060
Change-Id: I218dc93b1316b770245303c846f8f04a605817e7

to avoid fuzzer errors.

commit.subject: [fuzz] Specify visibility of LLVMFuzzerInitialize
commit.message:
[fuzz] Specify visibility of LLVMFuzzerInitialize

On macOS the linker appears to strip out this function, which then
means our command-line options have no affect. Specifying the
visibility seems to fix this.

Fix inspired by the V8 fuzzer which does this for the same reason.

Fixed: 456746910
Change-Id: I11233cb8aaaa60efbe91cc75e47511791c814bab

commit.subject: WebGPU Kotlin: Rename `kotlin_prefixed_name` method to `kotlin_name`
commit.message:
WebGPU Kotlin: Rename `kotlin_prefixed_name` method to `kotlin_name`

Since this function is the primary helper for generating all Kotlin type
names, not just prefixed ones, it is renamed to `kotlin_name` to more
accurately reflect its general purpose.

Change-Id: Ib00839b95bcc6455b011cdc35ffdf35ae132a4ff

parents.subject: [tint][benchmark] Use TINT_ASSERT instead of SkipWithError

commit.subject: [emscripten] Add missing header to (non-pkg) CMake build
commit.message:
[emscripten] Add missing header to (non-pkg) CMake build

The CMake build files for Emdawnwebgpu had a bug where they were relying
on the system include paths in Emscripten. Now that Emscripten has
removed USE_WEBGPU, there's no include path that includes
webgpu/webgpu_enum_class_bitmasks.h.

This shims that in via emdawnwebgpu_headers_gen, same as what we did
with webgpu_glfw.h.

The originally-reported bug can be reproduced by building
emdawnwebgpu_init_only_sample with CMake on Emscripten 4.0.18. (Will
roll that separately to make sure this works both before and after.)

Bug: https://github.com/beaufortfrancois/webgpu-cross-platform-app/issues/25
Change-Id: I8e15c3d629e36fec85d2443c941d1a2af532a437

parents.subject: [tint] Allow TINT_ASSERT to stream a custom error

commit.subject: [tint][benchmark] Use TINT_ASSERT instead of SkipWithError
commit.message:
[tint][benchmark] Use TINT_ASSERT instead of SkipWithError

SkipWithError does not result in a non-zero exit code. The benchmarks
should never fail to run, so we should just crash instead if these
conditions fail. This will allow us to add a benchmark run to our CQ
to ensure that we do not break the benchmarks again.

Bug: 445456830
Change-Id: Iee42a87d795d1f157fee7bf3461f74de8fe11d97

commit.subject: Polyfill unpack2x16{su}norm on Apple M3+ devices.
commit.message:
Polyfill unpack2x16{su}norm on Apple M3+ devices.

The unpack2x16{su}norm calls on M3+ devices appear to be returning the
wrong value when the source value comes from a non-4-byte aligned value.
It appears to be taking the original alignment of the word and using
that instead of the shifted values we're creating.

Fixed: 449576833
Change-Id: Iceeb2970eaad6aa969ace3c4db0a75187fa5a607

commit.subject: dawn_end2end_tests: fix tests that were assuming "use_dxc" was not the default
commit.message:
dawn_end2end_tests: fix tests that were assuming "use_dxc" was not the default

This fixes the following tests, which would run these tests twice on DXC
by default, and twice on FXC if `use_dxc` was disabled by command line:

- SinglePipelineCachingTests
- ComputeLayoutMemoryBufferTests
- ShaderF16Tests
- ShaderTests

Also fix up a couple of other tests to normalize the pattern to:
```
D3D12Backend(),
D3D12Backend({}, {"use_dxc"}),
```

Bug: 454348977
Change-Id: Ib86b0df286f293ddeafe16f93f29e0ead68b7766

commit.subject: GL: add AddGLWork() to defer GL commands
commit.message:
GL: add AddGLWork() to defer GL commands

This a foundation to support multithreading in future. To support
multithreading, we will defer the GL commands and execute them later in
a centrialized function called ExecuteGLWorks(). This will ensure that
only one thread can trigger ExecuteGLWorks() at a time.

Bug: 451928481
Change-Id: Ie1e10fc0a7d8e5b263740c2d1201d231cf52a6c3

commit.message:
GL: add AddGLWork() to defer GL commands

This a foundation to support multithreading in future. To support
multithreading, we will defer the GL commands and execute them later in
a centrialized function called ExecutePendingGLWork(). This will ensure
that only one thread can trigger ExecutePendingGLWork() at a time.

commit.subject: GL: add AddGLWork() to defer GL commands
commit.message:
GL: add AddGLWork() to defer GL commands

This a foundation to support multithreading in future. To support
multithreading, we will defer the GL commands and execute them later in
a centrialized function called ExecutePendingGLWork(). This will ensure
that only one thread can trigger ExecutePendingGLWork() at a time.

parents.subject: Make DynamicUploader acquire device mutex on submit

commit.subject: Add assertion device mutex is locked
commit.message:
Add assertion device mutex is locked

DynamicUploader::UpdateMemoryPendingSubmit() requires that device mutex
is locked for synchronization. Add as assertion to verify that. Also
lock mutex from DeviceD3D12::Initialize() since it creates a buffer.

Bug: 454667734
Change-Id: If4fde36a7d785fcd6603af848b2358310cf988be

commit.subject: [cmake] Only require DXC for fuzzers if being explcitly built
commit.message:
[cmake] Only require DXC for fuzzers if being explcitly built

Matches GN behaviour,
https://dawn-review.googlesource.com/c/dawn/+/239254, and prevents
unexpected linking issues when building HLSL writer.

Fixes: 455606190
Change-Id: Iea616167c9f75a983998afe79a6da07865ef7bf6

commit.subject: Add `GPU` prefix to Kotlin object classes
commit.message:
Add `GPU` prefix to Kotlin object classes

This change updates the Kotlin code generator to prefix all generated
WebGPU object types (e.g., `Device`, `Adapter`, `Texture`) with "GPU".

The implementation involves:
* Modifying `dawn_json_generator.py` to conditionally add "GPU"
    to the class name when the entity category is 'object'.
* Adding `kotlin_prefixed_name` and `jni_prefixed_name` macros to the
    generator templates to handle the new names.
* Updating all generator templates (.kt, .cpp) to use these macros
    for class definitions, JNI lookups, and type signatures.
* Updating the `BUILD.gn` file to reflect the new generated
    filenames (e.g., `Adapter.kt` is now `GPUAdapter.kt`).
* Updating existing Kotlin helper and test files to use the new class
    names.

Change-Id: I3983919a5eb543871c8c1b00104d6b52e60e6f95

commit.subject: vulkan: Initialize mappable buffers with memset()
commit.message:
vulkan: Initialize mappable buffers with memset()

This avoids having to issue vkCmdFillBuffer() which requires barriers and delays when MapAsync() returns to after command completion.

commit.subject: dawn.node: fix false positive failures when required features are not supported by the adapter
commit.message:
dawn.node: fix false positive failures when required features are not supported by the adapter

Fixes failures like:
```
webgpu:api,operation,adapter,requestDevice:features,known:feature="texture-compression-etc2" [fail]
  - EXPECTATION FAILED: THREW OperationError, instead of TypeError: OperationError: Invalid feature required: Requested feature FeatureName::TextureCompressionETC2 is not supported.
```

We need to throw TypeError when require feature is not supported by the adapter as per:
https://gpuweb.github.io/gpuweb/#dom-gpuadapter-requestdevice:~:text=Reject%20promise%20with%20a%20TypeError

Bug: None
Change-Id: Ibbe0c21c4286122f6b88ff711a486fbd30e26acf

commit.subject: dawn.node: fix false positive failures when required features are not supported by the adapter
commit.message:
dawn.node: fix false positive failures when required features are not supported by the adapter

Fixes failures like:
```
webgpu:api,operation,adapter,requestDevice:features,known:feature="texture-compression-etc2" [fail]
  - EXPECTATION FAILED: THREW OperationError, instead of TypeError: OperationError: Invalid feature required: Requested feature FeatureName::TextureCompressionETC2 is not supported.
```

We need to throw TypeError when require feature is not supported by the adapter as per:
https://gpuweb.github.io/gpuweb/#dom-gpuadapter-requestdevice:~:text=Reject%20promise%20with%20a%20TypeError

commit.subject: [spirv-reader] Convert access results for atomics.
commit.message:
[spirv-reader] Convert access results for atomics.

When converting an `Access` chain which is being turned into an atomic,
the result of the access also needs to be updated as those uses of the
access chain need to be atomics.

Fixed: 455844215

commit.subject: Make DynamicUploader acquire device mutex on submit
commit.message:
Make DynamicUploader acquire device mutex on submit

This will allow calling MaybeSubmitPendingCommands() without first
holding the device mutex. mMemoryPendingSubmit is made atomic. All
access to is using memory_order_relaxed since the device mutex provides
synchronization between threads already.

Also add an additional check of the pending command serial inside
MaybeSubmitPendingCommands(). It's possible a queue submit happened on
another thread and mMemoryPendingSubmit() should actually be zero. In
that case a queue submit would be attempted when it's not needed.

Bug: 454667734
Change-Id: I91dae13b55c26a1fc163cd9179fc73d0388814fb

commit.subject: [tint] Fix writer benchmarks for multiple entry points
commit.message:
[tint] Fix writer benchmarks for multiple entry points

All of the backends now assume that there is only one entry point, so
run `SingleEntryPoint` before invoking the backend.

The GLSL backend already did this.
